Press Release article 3 of 5, 296 words

September 30 2025: TXNM Energy: File SEC Form 10-Q - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-Q

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

The following Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations for TXNM is presented on a combined basis, including certain information applicable to PNM and TNMP. The MD&A for PNM and TNMP is presented as permitted by Form 10-Q General Instruction H(2). This report uses the term "Company" when discussing matters of common applicability to TXNM, PNM, and TNMP. A reference to a "Note" in this Item 2 refers to the accompanying Notes to Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements (Unaudited) included in Item 1, unless otherwise specified. Certain of the tables below may not appear visually accurate due to rounding.

MD&A FOR TXNM

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Overview

TXNM is a holding company with two regulated electric utilities, PNM and TNMP, serving approximately 839,000 residential, commercial, and industrial customers in New Mexico and Texas. TXNM strives to create a clean and bright energy future for customers, communities, and shareholders. TXNM's strategy and decision-making are focused on safely providing reliable, affordable, and environmentally responsible power built on a foundation of sustainability.

Recent Developments

Merger

On May 18, 2025, TXNM, Parent, and Merger Sub (both Parent and Merger Sub are affiliates of Blackstone Infrastructure) entered into the Merger Agreement pursuant to which Merger Sub will merge with and into TXNM, with TXNM surviving the Merger as a direct, wholly-owned subsidiary of Parent. Pursuant to the Merger Agreement, each issued and outstanding share of the common stock of TXNM (other than those listed in Note 17) at the Effective Time will be converted into the right to receive $61.25 in cash, without interest.

The proposed Merger has been unanimously approved by the Board.

Press Release article 4 of 5, Source: WEC, 78 words

September 25 2025: TXNM Energy quarterly dividends steady

TXNM Energy today announced a quarterly dividend of 40.75c per share for Q3/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q2/2025) dividend of 40.75c. The ex-dividend date is Friday, October 24, 2025 and the record date is Friday, October 24, 2025 and it is payable on Friday, November 14. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended September 25 were $1.61.

Press Release article 5 of 5, Source: Company Website, 317 words

August 28 2025: TXNM Energy Shareholders Overwhelmingly Approve Acquisition by Blackstone Infrastructure

TXNM Energy (NYSE: TXNM) shareholders voted overwhelmingly to approve the agreement under which Blackstone Infrastructure will acquire TXNM Energy at a special shareholders meeting held earlier today. Under the terms of the proposed agreement, TXNM Energy shareholders will receive $61.25 in cash for each share of TXNM Energy common stock held at closing.

Of the shares voted, 99.6 percent voted to approve the proposed agreement, reflecting 88.2 percent of shares issued and outstanding on the record date. TXNM Energy will report the final vote results in a Current Report on Form 8-K to be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Regulatory approvals from the New Mexico Public Regulation Commission, Public Utility Commission of Texas, Federal Energy Regulatory Commission, Department of Justice (Hart Scott-Rodino Clearance), Nuclear Regulatory Commission and Federal Communications Commission are being pursued.

TXNM Energy continues to anticipate that the closing of the acquisition will occur in the second half of 2026, subject to the satisfaction or waiver of the customary closing conditions, including among other things, receipt of required state and federal regulatory approvals.

May 12th, 2026 ( Zolmax.com - Delivered by Newstex )

Tesla, IREN, and Coterra Energy are the three Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, refining, transporting, or supplying energy, such as oil, natural gas, coal, and renewable power. For stock market investors, this sector includes businesses like major oil producers, pipeline operators, utility companies, and clean energy firms, and their performance is often influenced by commodity prices, regulation, and global demand for energy. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla (TSLA)

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

IREN (IREN)

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

Coterra Energy (CTRA)

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

May 12th, 2026 ( Zolmax.com - Delivered by Newstex )

TXNM Energy ( NYSE:TXNM - Get Free Report) and Ameresco ( NYSE:AMRC - Get Free Report) are both energy companies, but which is the better stock? We will contrast the two companies based on the strength of their institutional ownership, earnings, dividends, profitability, analyst recommendations, valuation and risk.

Profitability

This table compares TXNM Energy and Ameresco's net margins, return on equity and return on assets.

Institutional & Insider Ownership

91.7% of TXNM Energy shares are owned by institutional investors. Comparatively, 99.2% of Ameresco shares are owned by institutional investors. 1.1% of TXNM Energy shares are owned by company insiders. Comparatively, 45.0% of Ameresco shares are owned by company insiders. Strong institutional ownership is an indication that hedge funds, endowments and large money managers believe a stock will outperform the market over the long term.

Valuation & Earnings

This table compares TXNM Energy and Ameresco"s top-line revenue, earnings per share (EPS) and valuation.

TXNM Energy has higher revenue and earnings than Ameresco. TXNM Energy is trading at a lower price-to-earnings ratio than Ameresco, indicating that it is currently the more affordable of the two stocks.

Volatility & Risk

TXNM Energy has a beta of 0.16, indicating that its share price is 84% less volatile than the S&P 500. Comparatively, Ameresco has a beta of 2.55, indicating that its share price is 155% more volatile than the S&P 500.

Analyst Ratings

This is a summary of current recommendations for TXNM Energy and Ameresco, as provided by MarketBeat.com.

TXNM Energy presently has a consensus target price of $60.88, suggesting a potential upside of 2.73%. Ameresco has a consensus target price of $42.11, suggesting a potential upside of 43.82%. Given Ameresco's stronger consensus rating and higher possible upside, analysts plainly believe Ameresco is more favorable than TXNM Energy.

About TXNM Energy

( Get Free Report)

TXNM Energy, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides electricity and electric services in the United States. It operates through Public Service Company of New Mexico (PNM) and Texas-New Mexico Power Company (TNMP) segments. The PNM segment engages in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. The segment owns and leases communications, office and other equipment, office space, vehicles, and real estate. It generates electricity using coal, natural gas and oil, and nuclear fuel and waste, as well as solar, wind, geothermal, and battery storage energy sources. The TNMP segment provides regulated transmission and distribution services. The segment also owns and leases vehicles, service facilities, and office locations throughout its service territory. The company serves residential, commercial, and industrial customers and end-users of electricity in New Mexico and Texas. The company was formerly known as PNM Resources, Inc and changed its name to TXNM Energy, Inc. in August 2024. TXNM Energy, Inc. was founded in 1882 and is based in Albuquerque, New Mexico.

About Ameresco

( Get Free Report)

Ameresco, Inc., a clean technology integrator, provides a portfolio of energy efficiency and renewable energy supply solutions in the United States, Canada, Europe, and internationally. It operates through U.S. Regions, U.S. Federal, Canada, Europe, Alternative Fuels, and All Other segments. The company offers energy efficiency, infrastructure upgrades, energy security and resilience, asset sustainability, and renewable energy solutions for businesses and organizations. It designs, develops, engineers, and installs projects that reduce the energy, as well as operations and maintenance (O&M) costs of its customers' facilities; and projects primarily include various measures customized for the facility and designed to enhance the efficiency of building systems, such as heating, ventilation, cooling, and lighting systems. In addition, the company offers renewable energy solutions and services, such as the construction of small-scale plants that the company owns or develops for customers that produce electricity, gas, heat, or cooling from renewable sources of energy and O&M services; and electricity, processed renewable gas fuel, and heat or cooling produced from renewable sources of energy. Further, the company sells photovoltaic (PV) solar energy products and systems, as well as provides consulting, and enterprise energy management services; and operates wind farms. It serves the federal, state, local governments, healthcare and educational institutions, airports, public housing authorities and public universities, municipal utilities, and commercial and industrial customers. As of December 31, 2023, the company owned and operated 185 small-scale renewable energy plants and solar PV installations. Ameresco, Inc. was incorporated in 2000 and is headquartered in Framingham, Massachusetts.

Link to the original story.

urn:contentItem:6JK4-0TW3-S3VK-N4NX-00000-00Energy Stocks To Follow Today - May 12th2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisEnergy Stocks To Follow Today - May 12th

Tesla, IREN, and Coterra Energy are the three Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, refining, transporting, or supplying energy, such as oil, natural gas, coal, and renewable power. For stock market investors, this sector includes businesses like major oil producers, pipeline operators, utility companies, and clean energy firms, and their performance is often influenced by commodity prices, regulation, and global demand for energy. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla TSLA

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

IREN IREN

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

Coterra Energy CTRA

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

value.Title: Siemens Energy May Gain Talent-Mobility Edge Following GE Vernova's Mauze v. GE Vernova Oregon Retaliation Challenge *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-S553-S8GP-024W-00000-00Siemens Energy May Gain Talent-Mobility Edge Following GE Vernova's Mauze v. GE Vernova Oregon Retaliation Challenge *Updated*2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisSiemens Energy May Gain Talent-Mobility Edge Following GE Vernova's Mauze v. GE Vernova Oregon Retaliation Challenge *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

What Stakeholders Need To Know

This situation demonstrates that post-employment limitations in the renewable energy market may lead to claims of whistleblower retaliation and economic interference under applicable state laws. Organizations encounter potential legal exposure when former staff members move to competing firms, particularly following safety reports in hazardous field environments. The results of such disputes may initiate industry-wide assessments of separation procedures, facility access guidelines, and whistleblower protections, potentially increasing compliance expenditures and altering recruitment methodologies. Wider implications might involve stricter operational standards for overseeing workforce transitions and competitor agreements, influencing flexibility across the power generation sector.

Background of the Case

The Otto Candies litigation originated in 2016 following the 2014 dissolution of Oceanografia S.A. de C.V., a Mexican oil-services contractor. Reports indicate that Oceanografia presented thousands of falsified Pemex receivables to Citigroup's subsidiary, Grupo Financiero Banamex, which advanced approximately $585 million against those invoices under a vendor-finance program. When Mexican officials suspended Oceanografia in February 2014, Banamex recognized a loss of $400 million. Institutional investors, including hedge funds, insurance firms, and pension plans that had acquired short-term Banamex-issued certificates, incurred principal losses estimated at $300 million. Claimants assert that Citigroup Inc. and senior executives based in New York knowingly participated in or recklessly disregarded a multi-year enterprise, allegedly violating civil RICO statutes and the Securities Exchange Act. While the case faced multiple dismissals based on statute-of-limitations and extraterritoriality, the 11th Circuit reversed these decisions in September 2024, ruling that sufficient domestic activity-such as New York approvals, U.S. dollar wire transfers, and regulatory filings-occurred to apply RICO domestically. Citigroup filed a petition for review in November 2024, which remains pending throughout 2025.

Correction regarding the applicable case parameters:

The relevant employment litigation, Mauze v. GE Vernova Inc. et al., No. 6:24-cv-01800 (D. Or.), was filed October 25, 2024. Plaintiff Sean A. Mauze alleges whistleblower retaliation under ORS § 659A.199 after reporting safety issues during a 2024 turbine blade replacement at the Border Lands wind farm in New Mexico. Following termination on May 1, 2024, Mauze joined competitor Clean Energy Services, Inc., only for GE Vernova to impose a site-access ban communicated in July 2024, resulting in his separation from the new role. Claims include retaliation, intentional interference with economic relations, and blacklisting. On April 17, 2025, the court denied the motion to dismiss in part (retaliation and IIER claims survive) and granted it in part (blacklisting dismissed). The case remains active with discovery underway.

Competitive Impacts on Siemens Energy

Siemens Energy's international wind and service units might experience indirect recruitment advantages due to the perception of increased employment restrictions at GE Vernova facilities. Industry observations indicate a growing preference among field technicians for employers offering transparent labor mobility frameworks. This environment might gradually redirect workforce availability toward Siemens Energy's operations across North America and Europe.

Possible Opportunities for Siemens Energy

Accelerated hiring of experienced wind-turbine personnel formerly associated with GE Vernova operations.

Improved standing in joint-venture proposals where workforce consistency serves as a qualitative factor.

Increased acquisition of service agreements for North American wind installations by highlighting accommodating post-employment policies.

Fortified corporate branding initiatives emphasizing comprehensive safety-reporting protections.

Why This Case Is Challenging for Siemens Energy

Although not directly involved, the continuation of retaliation and interference claims in this dispute might generate industry-wide regulatory and investor examination regarding post-employment guidelines. Siemens Energy may face comparable assessments of its facility-access and vendor procedures, resulting in increased compliance audits and necessary revisions to recruitment materials.

Key Challenges for Siemens Energy

Industry-wide increases in whistleblower auditing requirements and policy standardization expenses.

Additional inquiries from investors and market analysts regarding workforce retention and mobility structures.

Potential indirect legal exposure if comparable operational patterns occur within Siemens Energy facilities.

Increased considerations for legal reserves and insurance premiums related to employment practices in the domestic renewable sector.

Competitor's Strategic Responses

Executed internal evaluations of facility-access and separation procedures against evolving legal standards in Oregon.

Revised corporate employee manuals to include explicit whistleblower protections and post-employment communication parameters.

Initiated specific recruitment campaigns directed at wind-service specialists, emphasizing commitments to professional mobility.

Retained external legal counsel to perform comprehensive compliance assessments across North American regional operations.

Key Measures

Siemens Energy is executing these strategies via interdepartmental committees that report to senior human resources executives, facilitating efficient incorporation into worldwide operations while sustaining established project schedules.

Possible Losses and Unexpected Advantages

Potential Advantages

Expansion of market share in wind-service talent recruitment, which may support additional revenue generation.

Enhanced corporate reputation that could improve competitiveness during the bidding process for renewable energy projects.

Expedited project deployment capabilities within constrained labor environments.

Improved environmental, social, and governance evaluations resulting from validated fair employment practices.

Potential Losses

Aggregate expenses associated with compliance activities and policy revisions across international operations.

Temporary reallocation of administrative resources away from primary technological development objectives.

Increased insurance costs associated with employment-practice liabilities.

Short-term operational margin adjustments caused by expedited recruitment and training expenditures.

Final Thoughts

Siemens Energy has the capacity to leverage current market conditions by consolidating its status as a leading employer in the renewable sector while strengthening internal risk management against comparable disputes. A calibrated strategy that aligns market opportunities with rigorous compliance protocols enables the organization to manage industry-wide adjustments in employment standards, thereby sustaining its position in wind energy services.

GE Vernova share has been a three bagger in the past year. A year ago the GE Vernova share price was $415.72. $1,000 would have bought 2.4 GE Vernova shares on that day. Those 2.4 Shares would be worth $2,579 at today's share price of $1,071.98. Dividends reinvested are worth $1. PV$1000= $2,580.

Activities

GE Vernova, a spin-off from General Electric, is a global leader in the energy sector, focusing on innovative solutions for power generation, renewable energy, and electrification. The company's core activities encompass the design, manufacturing, and servicing of technologies for gas and steam power plants, wind turbines, hydroelectric systems, and grid management solutions, enabling reliable and efficient energy delivery worldwide. Its fastest-growing segments include offshore wind and electrification technologies, driven by rising demand for sustainable energy sources amid global decarbonization efforts. Additionally, GE Vernova leverages advanced digital platforms for predictive maintenance and asset optimization, enhancing operational efficiency for utilities and industrial clients. With a strong presence in markets like the U.S. NYSE, the company is positioned to capitalize on the transition to cleaner energy, supporting infrastructure development and energy security. It is the NYSE's largest Energy company by market capitalisation.

GE Vernova (NYSE:GEV) is poised as a leading force in the renewable energy sector, leveraging its expertise as a premier wind turbine manufacturer to drive sustainable progress globally. Central to its operations are the GE Vernova wind turbines, including advanced models like the GE Vernova Haliade-X 14MW and the GE Vernova Cypress onshore turbine. Strategic projects, such as the GE Vernova wind turbine supply Spain and the GE Vernova Spain wind project, underscore its robust market presence and capability in executing large-scale GE Vernova wind farm supply contracts. By focusing on innovative solutions like GE Vernova floating offshore turbines, GE Vernova grid connection solutions, and comprehensive GE Vernova O&M services, the company ensures superior turbine performance and efficiency. With a commitment to excellence, evidenced by the GE Vernova wind turbine maintenance and GE Vernova turbine commissioning, GE Vernova is strategically positioned for long-term growth, especially in green energy in Spain.

urn:contentItem:6JHR-TJ33-RTHD-V4WH-00000-00Top Energy Stocks To Keep An Eye On - May 7th2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisTop Energy Stocks To Keep An Eye On - May 7th

TSLA: 3 Reasons the Stock Could Hit $400 in May

Tesla, Coterra Energy, IREN, AltC Acquisition, Bloom Energy, GE Vernova, and onsemi are the seven Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, transporting, refining, or supporting energy-including oil and gas explorers and producers, pipelines, utilities, renewable energy developers, and equipment and service firms. For investors, these stocks tend to be sensitive to commodity prices, supply and demand dynamics, and regulation, and can offer either dividend income or growth potential depending on the company's business and exposure to the energy transition. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla TSLA

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Tesla's Cybercab Is Finally Real-But Is It Enough?

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

Coterra Energy CTRA

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

MarketBeat Week in Review - 04/20 - 04/24

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

IREN IREN

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

AltC Acquisition ALCC

AltC Acquisition Corp. does not have significant operations. It intends to effect a merger, capital stock exchange, asset acquisition, stock purchase, reorganization, or other business combination with one or more businesses. The company was formerly known as Churchill Capital Corp VIII and changed its name to AltC Acquisition Corp.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ALCC

Bloom Energy BE

Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid-oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. The company offers Bloom Energy Server, a solid oxide technology that converts fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels into electricity through an electrochemical process without combustion.

Read Our Latest Research Report on BE

GE Vernova GEV

GE Vernova LLC, an energy business company, generates electricity. It operates under three segments: Power, Wind, and Electrification. The Power segments generates and sells electricity through hydro, gas, nuclear, and steam power. Wind segment engages in the manufacturing and sale of wind turbine blades; and Electrification segment provides grid solutions, power conversion, solar, and storage solutions.

Read Our Latest Research Report on GEV

onsemi ON

onsemi is engaged in disruptive innovations and also a supplier of power and analog semiconductors. The firm offers vehicle electrification and safety, sustainable energy grids, industrial automation, and 5G and cloud infrastructure, with a focus on automotive and industrial end-markets. It operates through the following segments: Power Solutions Group, Advanced Solutions Group, and Intelligent Sensing Group.

May 6th, 2026 ( Transcript Daily - Delivered by Newstex )

Tesla, Coterra Energy, IREN, AltC Acquisition, Bloom Energy, GE Vernova, and onsemi are the seven Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, transporting, refining, or supporting energyincluding oil and gas explorers and producers, pipelines, utilities, renewable energy developers, and equipment and service firms. For investors, these stocks tend to be sensitive to commodity prices, supply and demand dynamics, and regulation, and can offer either dividend income or growth potential depending on the company's business and exposure to the energy transition. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla (TSLA)

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

Coterra Energy (CTRA)

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

IREN (IREN)

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

AltC Acquisition (ALCC)

AltC Acquisition Corp. does not have significant operations. It intends to effect a merger, capital stock exchange, asset acquisition, stock purchase, reorganization, or other business combination with one or more businesses. The company was formerly known as Churchill Capital Corp VIII and changed its name to AltC Acquisition Corp.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ALCC

Bloom Energy (BE)

Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid-oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. The company offers Bloom Energy Server, a solid oxide technology that converts fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels into electricity through an electrochemical process without combustion.

Read Our Latest Research Report on BE

GE Vernova (GEV)

GE Vernova LLC, an energy business company, generates electricity. It operates under three segments: Power, Wind, and Electrification. The Power segments generates and sells electricity through hydro, gas, nuclear, and steam power. Wind segment engages in the manufacturing and sale of wind turbine blades; and Electrification segment provides grid solutions, power conversion, solar, and storage solutions.

Read Our Latest Research Report on GEV

onsemi (ON)

onsemi is engaged in disruptive innovations and also a supplier of power and analog semiconductors. The firm offers vehicle electrification and safety, sustainable energy grids, industrial automation, and 5G and cloud infrastructure, with a focus on automotive and industrial end-markets. It operates through the following segments: Power Solutions Group, Advanced Solutions Group, and Intelligent Sensing Group.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ON

May 9th, 2026 ( DailyPolitical - Delivered by Newstex )

Tesla, IREN, Bloom Energy, ExxonMobil, Coterra Energy, Chevron, and GE Vernova are the seven Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, exploring, refining, transporting, or distributing energy resources such as oil, natural gas, and electricity. For stock market investors, the term usually refers to companies in sectors like oil and gas, utilities, and renewable energy that can offer returns tied to energy prices, demand, and regulatory changes. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla (TSLA)

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

IREN (IREN)

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

Bloom Energy (BE)

Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid-oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. The company offers Bloom Energy Server, a solid oxide technology that converts fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels into electricity through an electrochemical process without combustion.

Read Our Latest Research Report on BE

ExxonMobil (XOM)

ExxonMobil Corporation engages in the exploration and production of crude oil and natural gas in the United States and internationally. It operates through Upstream, Energy Products, Chemical Products, and Specialty Products segments. The Upstream segment explores for and produces crude oil and natural gas.

Read Our Latest Research Report on XOM

Coterra Energy (CTRA)

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

Chevron (CVX)

Chevron Corporation, through its subsidiaries, engages in the integrated energy and chemicals operations in the United States and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Upstream and Downstream. The Upstream segment is involved in the exploration, development, production, and transportation of crude oil and natural gas; processing, liquefaction, transportation, and regasification of liquefied natural gas; transportation of crude oil through pipelines; transportation, storage, and marketing of natural gas; and carbon capture and storage, as well as a gas-to-liquids plant.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CVX

GE Vernova (GEV)

GE Vernova LLC, an energy business company, generates electricity. It operates under three segments: Power, Wind, and Electrification. The Power segments generates and sells electricity through hydro, gas, nuclear, and steam power. Wind segment engages in the manufacturing and sale of wind turbine blades; and Electrification segment provides grid solutions, power conversion, solar, and storage solutions.

Read Our Latest Research Report on GEV

May 9th, 2026 ( Zolmax.com - Delivered by Newstex )

Tesla, IREN, Bloom Energy, ExxonMobil, Coterra Energy, Chevron, and GE Vernova are the seven Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, exploring, refining, transporting, or distributing energy resources such as oil, natural gas, and electricity. For stock market investors, the term usually refers to companies in sectors like oil and gas, utilities, and renewable energy that can offer returns tied to energy prices, demand, and regulatory changes. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla (TSLA)

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

IREN (IREN)

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

Bloom Energy (BE)

Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid-oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. The company offers Bloom Energy Server, a solid oxide technology that converts fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels into electricity through an electrochemical process without combustion.

Read Our Latest Research Report on BE

ExxonMobil (XOM)

ExxonMobil Corporation engages in the exploration and production of crude oil and natural gas in the United States and internationally. It operates through Upstream, Energy Products, Chemical Products, and Specialty Products segments. The Upstream segment explores for and produces crude oil and natural gas.

Read Our Latest Research Report on XOM

Coterra Energy (CTRA)

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

Chevron (CVX)

Chevron Corporation, through its subsidiaries, engages in the integrated energy and chemicals operations in the United States and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Upstream and Downstream. The Upstream segment is involved in the exploration, development, production, and transportation of crude oil and natural gas; processing, liquefaction, transportation, and regasification of liquefied natural gas; transportation of crude oil through pipelines; transportation, storage, and marketing of natural gas; and carbon capture and storage, as well as a gas-to-liquids plant.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CVX

GE Vernova (GEV)

GE Vernova LLC, an energy business company, generates electricity. It operates under three segments: Power, Wind, and Electrification. The Power segments generates and sells electricity through hydro, gas, nuclear, and steam power. Wind segment engages in the manufacturing and sale of wind turbine blades; and Electrification segment provides grid solutions, power conversion, solar, and storage solutions.

Read Our Latest Research Report on GEV

urn:contentItem:6JJC-P8X3-RWM8-M4DS-00000-00Promising Energy Stocks To Add to Your Watchlist - May 10th2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisPromising Energy Stocks To Add to Your Watchlist - May 10th

TSLA: 3 Reasons the Stock Could Hit $400 in May

Tesla, IREN, Bloom Energy, ExxonMobil, Coterra Energy, Chevron, and GE Vernova are the seven Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, exploring, refining, transporting, or distributing energy resources such as oil, natural gas, and electricity. For stock market investors, the term usually refers to companies in sectors like oil and gas, utilities, and renewable energy that can offer returns tied to energy prices, demand, and regulatory changes. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla TSLA

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Tesla's Cybercab Is Finally Real-But Is It Enough?

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

IREN IREN

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

MarketBeat Week in Review - 04/20 - 04/24

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

Bloom Energy BE

Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid-oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. The company offers Bloom Energy Server, a solid oxide technology that converts fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels into electricity through an electrochemical process without combustion.

Read Our Latest Research Report on BE

ExxonMobil XOM

ExxonMobil Corporation engages in the exploration and production of crude oil and natural gas in the United States and internationally. It operates through Upstream, Energy Products, Chemical Products, and Specialty Products segments. The Upstream segment explores for and produces crude oil and natural gas.

Read Our Latest Research Report on XOM

Coterra Energy CTRA

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

ChevronCVX

Chevron Corporation, through its subsidiaries, engages in the integrated energy and chemicals operations in the United States and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Upstream and Downstream. The Upstream segment is involved in the exploration, development, production, and transportation of crude oil and natural gas; processing, liquefaction, transportation, and regasification of liquefied natural gas; transportation of crude oil through pipelines; transportation, storage, and marketing of natural gas; and carbon capture and storage, as well as a gas-to-liquids plant.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CVX

GE Vernova GEV

GE Vernova LLC, an energy business company, generates electricity. It operates under three segments: Power, Wind, and Electrification. The Power segments generates and sells electricity through hydro, gas, nuclear, and steam power. Wind segment engages in the manufacturing and sale of wind turbine blades; and Electrification segment provides grid solutions, power conversion, solar, and storage solutions.

urn:contentItem:6JJ2-5MJ3-RW76-P220-00000-00Top Energy Stocks To Keep An Eye On - May 7th2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisTop Energy Stocks To Keep An Eye On - May 7th

Tesla, Coterra Energy, IREN, AltC Acquisition, Bloom Energy, GE Vernova, and onsemi are the seven Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, transporting, refining, or supporting energy-including oil and gas explorers and producers, pipelines, utilities, renewable energy developers, and equipment and service firms. For investors, these stocks tend to be sensitive to commodity prices, supply and demand dynamics, and regulation, and can offer either dividend income or growth potential depending on the company's business and exposure to the energy transition. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla TSLA

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

Coterra Energy CTRA

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

IREN IREN

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

AltC Acquisition ALCC

AltC Acquisition Corp. does not have significant operations. It intends to effect a merger, capital stock exchange, asset acquisition, stock purchase, reorganization, or other business combination with one or more businesses. The company was formerly known as Churchill Capital Corp VIII and changed its name to AltC Acquisition Corp.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ALCC

Bloom Energy BE

Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid-oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. The company offers Bloom Energy Server, a solid oxide technology that converts fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels into electricity through an electrochemical process without combustion.

Read Our Latest Research Report on BE

GE Vernova GEV

GE Vernova LLC, an energy business company, generates electricity. It operates under three segments: Power, Wind, and Electrification. The Power segments generates and sells electricity through hydro, gas, nuclear, and steam power. Wind segment engages in the manufacturing and sale of wind turbine blades; and Electrification segment provides grid solutions, power conversion, solar, and storage solutions.

Read Our Latest Research Report on GEV

onsemi ON

onsemi is engaged in disruptive innovations and also a supplier of power and analog semiconductors. The firm offers vehicle electrification and safety, sustainable energy grids, industrial automation, and 5G and cloud infrastructure, with a focus on automotive and industrial end-markets. It operates through the following segments: Power Solutions Group, Advanced Solutions Group, and Intelligent Sensing Group.

Read Our Latest Research Report on ON

urn:contentItem:6JJV-5033-RYHP-414K-00000-00SunState Solar Provides Access to Affordable Clean Energy Solutions in Albuquerque and Santa Fe2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisNEWSSunState Solar Provides Access to Affordable Clean Energy Solutions in Albuquerque and Santa FeAlbuquerque, New Mexico, United States, May

SunState Solar is helping homeowners in Albuquerque transition to cost-saving, environmentally friendly energy with customized solar installations and flexible financing options. With local expertise, 5-star service, and comprehensive support from consultation to installation, the company makes switching to solar simple and accessible. -- According to the Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA), solar accounted for 54%...

SunState Solar is helping homeowners in Albuquerque transition to cost-saving, environmentally friendly energy with customized solar installations and flexible financing options. With local expertise, 5-star service, and comprehensive support from consulta...

SunState Solar is helping homeowners in Albuquerque transition to cost-saving, environmentally friendly energy with customized solar installations and flexible financing options. With local expertise, 5-star service, and comprehensive support from consultation to installation, the company makes switching to solar simple and accessible. -- According to the Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA), solar accounted for 54% of all new electricity-generating capacity added to the US grid in 2025. Solar and storage made up 79% of new capacity in the same year, and SEIA expects cumulative US solar capacity to nearly triple from year-end 2025 to 2036. As more people are becoming aware of the planet's climate crisis, they're increasingly turning to renewable energy that leaves as small a carbon footprint as possible. As shown in the above statistics, solar energy is a particularly popular option, as it can be cleanly harvested from a natural resource. Solar energy should be something for homeowners in Albuquerque to consider, especially with recent rises in energy costs. While there are significant upfront costs to consider, ultimately, going solar can drastically lower utility bills, especially when combined with tax credits and incentives (e.g., 30% federal solar investment tax credit (ITC), as well as state and local incentives). For many people, the systems pay for themselves within just a few years; homeowners can lock in low energy costs for 25+ years, too. On top of that, they enjoy net meter benefits, where they sell excess energy back to the grid for credits. is here to provide installation services for both homeowners and businesses, and these services are backed by its 100% 5-star ratings on Google and other platforms. Every system comes with a 25-year warranty and worry-free installation, as the business is committed to delivering top-quality solar solutions. This is a locally owned and community-focused business, which sets them apart from large and impersonal solar companies. SunState Solar exclusively serves the Albuquerque and Santa Fe metro areas, which means that the team thoroughly knows local energy needs. The company also gives back to the community through charitable contributions, community involvement, and partnerships that create lasting positive change. Homeowners who need financial assistance with the upfront cost can utilize SunStates Solar's zero-down financing and flexible payment plans. For example, they can benefit from $0 down payment plans, as well as low/affordable monthly payments. They can also make use of the company's many financing partners. Interested parties can take advantage of SunState Solar's free step-by-step guide to understanding everything solar. This can help them make well-informed decisions confidently when switching to solar. They can also schedule a free consultation with this business. During this meeting, they'll go over current energy bills and energy needs by assessing the roof and energy usage. With this information, the company can design a custom solar system that meets and exceeds those requirements. Once the installation starts, it's all in SunState Solar's hands. Its team will handle the permits and paperwork, making it a stress-free process for homeowners. Other services that SunState Solar offers are solar inspections, pest barriers, solar panel cleaning, troubleshooting and repair, and solar monitoring. About the company: SunState Solar is a locally owned and operated solar installation company based in New Mexico, providing residential and commercial solar solutions throughout Albuquerque, Santa Fe, and surrounding areas.

urn:contentItem:6JJX-XY03-RT2N-14Y4-00000-00Western states are planning power demand a day ahead. Here’s why.2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:25ZLexisNexisNEWSWestern states are planning power demand a day ahead. Here’s why.Kaleb Roedel

The system is called the Extended Day-Ahead Market, or EDAM. It builds on an existing real-time electricity trading market that has operated across the West since 2014. According to supporters of the...

The system is called the Extended Day-Ahead Market, or EDAM. It builds on an existing real-time electricity trading market that has operated across the West since 2014. According to supporters of the new system, that real-time market has already saved cust...

The system is called the Extended Day-Ahead Market, or EDAM. It builds on an existing real-time electricity trading market that has operated across the West since 2014. According to supporters of the new system, that real-time market has already saved customers in the region nearly $7 billion by allowing utilities to trade excess power in short time windows before demand spikes. The new market expands that concept by giving utilities more time to plan ahead. “When you have a day to plan, you know where the wind is going to be blowing, you know where the sun is going to be shining,” said Brian Turner with Advanced Energy United, an advocacy group focused on clean energy and grid modernization. “And you can turn up and down your other generation, the coal plants or the gas plants.” Turner said utilities can use that added lead time to better balance supply and demand throughout the region, especially during extreme weather events, when electricity use can surge. Supporters say EDAM could also help lower costs by allowing utilities to buy cheaper power from neighboring states instead of relying only on their own generation. Rocky Mountain Power — Wyoming’s largest utility — is among the first participants in the new market, with additional Western utilities expected to join in the coming years. The launch comes as utilities across the Mountain West face growing electricity demand, rising summer temperatures and increasing pressure to integrate more renewable energy sources like wind and solar into the grid. This story was produced by the Mountain West News Bureau, a collaboration between KUNR, Wyoming Public Media, Nevada Public Radio, Boise State Public Radio in Idaho, KUNC in Northern Colorado, KANW in New Mexico, ColoradoPublic Radio, KJZZ in Arizona and NPR, with additional support from affiliate newsrooms across the region. Funding for the Mountain West News Bureau is provided in part by the Corporation for Public Broadcasting and Eric and Wendy Schmidt.
